strengths: 19+ years of software engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems, exceeding the 15-year minimum
Entrepreneurial track record: founded GISCollective (open-source geo-data platform) and sustained it since 2016 alongside employment
Recent hands-on AI-agent infrastructure work at re:cinq, including MCP code generators and frameworks for agentic workflows
concerns: No recognized AI/ML expertise or demonstrated AI-powered products or platforms at scale — AI exposure is limited to ~1 year of agent tooling
No evidence of director-level scope: career history is senior/staff IC delivery work, not organization-wide technical strategy or advising strategic customers
Cloud experience is practitioner-level (AWS CDK, Kubernetes deployments) rather than cloud platform strategy or future-state product direction

key_factors.factor: Recognized AI expertise and AI product track record

response.justification: The role's core competencies are recognized AI expertise with shipped AI products, cloud platform strategy, director-level influence, and entrepreneurial drive. The candidate clears the minimums: 19+ years of engineering (freelance 2006 through present) and a BS in Computer Science. Entrepreneurial experience is evidenced by founding GISCollective, and builder fluency is strong (DLang test tooling, performance systems at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/second). However, the preferred qualifications that define this role are largely absent: no advanced AI-related degree, no demonstrated AI products or platforms beyond recent MCP/agent tooling contributions at re:cinq, and no evidence of advising strategic customers or setting cloud product direction — his cloud work (AWS CDK, Kubernetes) is implementation-level. Public speaking (FOSS4G, workshops) shows knowledge-sharing ability but not recognized AI thought leadership. Strong senior/staff engineer, but a poor fit for a director-level AI strategy IC role.
